finding: Re-claiming an already amended task with `next --claim --claim-intent write` can rebuild the direction lock from original task scope and drop previously added scope-amendment paths, forcing the operator to re-run `tasks scope add`.
expectedBehavior: Re-claim should preserve active task scope amendments or clearly state that it is replacing the lock and list amendments that must be re-applied.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed in `packages/cli/src/commands/next.ts`: when re-claiming a task that already has an active claim, existing claim files are merged into the next allowed-files set so scope-amended paths survive intent escalation. Regression coverage in `scripts/validate-prompt-scoped-next.ts` creates `TASK-SCOPEAMEND-0001`, reclaims it from `closeout-only` to `write`, and asserts `src/scope-amend-linked.ts` remains in `claim.files`. Validation: `npm run validate:prompt-scoped-next` and `node --strip-types tests/cli/task-scope-amendment.test.ts`.
followUp: Keep the prompt-scoped next regression for `TASK-SCOPEAMEND-0001`; future claim-direction changes must preserve scope-amended paths across same-actor re-claim and intent upgrade.
